manual_entries.title: Default Zone Temperature Setpoints
manual_entries.summary: Default occupied heating/cooling setpoints: VAV zones 21°C/24°C; mechanical/electrical rooms 18°C/29°C; networking/computer 18°C/24°C. Unoccupied setpoints are also provided.

(AHU)  equipment schedules. They can also be generically listed by zone type, such as the example in (a) below.

manual_entries.title: TAB – Minimum Fan Speed Determination
manual_entries.summary: Determine minimum speed setpoints for VFD-driven equipment (supply, return, relief fans) by starting at 6 Hz (10%) and increasing until visibly rotating. That speed becomes the minimum speed setpoint.

structured_payload.title: TAB – Minimum Fan Speed Determination
structured_payload.summary: Determine minimum speed setpoints for VFD-driven equipment (supply, return, relief fans) by starting at 6 Hz (10%) and increasing until visibly rotating. That speed becomes the minimum speed setpoint.
structured_payload.commissioning_check: Verify that minimum speed setpoints are determined per TAB specifications: start fan at 6 Hz (10%), observe rotation, increase if not rotating, record speed as minimum setpoint.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Minimum speed setpoints for all VFD-driven equipment shall be determined in accordance with the testing, adjusting, and balancing (TAB) specifications for the following, as applicable: 1. Supply fan 2. Return fan 3. Relief fan. There needs to be corresponding instructions in the TAB specifications. For example: 1. Start the fan or pump. 2. Manually set speed to 6 Hz (10%), unless otherwise indicated in control sequences. For equipment with gear boxes, use whatever minimum speed is recommended by the tower manufacturer. 3. Observe the fan/pump in the field to ensure it is visibly rotating. If it is not, gradually increase speed until it is. 4. The speed at this point shall be the minimum speed setpoint for this piece of equipment.

The speed at this point shall be the minimum speed setpoint for this piece of equipment.

manual_entries.title: TAB – Minimum Outdoor Air DP Setpoint (Standard 62.1)
manual_entries.summary: Establish DesMinDP by opening minimum OA damper fully, closing economizer damper, measuring OA flow, adjusting damper linkage if needed, and recording DP across OA damper at design minimum flow.

structured_payload.title: TAB – Minimum Outdoor Air DP Setpoint (Standard 62.1)
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Multiple-zone VAV AHU with separate minimum OA damper and DP control
structured_payload.summary: Establish DesMinDP by opening minimum OA damper fully, closing economizer damper, measuring OA flow, adjusting damper linkage if needed, and recording DP across OA damper at design minimum flow.
structured_payload.commissioning_check: Verify DesMinDP is established per TAB: open minimum OA damper, close economizer damper, measure OA flow, adjust linkage if needed to achieve design minimum, record DP as DesMinDP.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Instructions for establishing MinDP are given in the TAB specification. For example: 1. Open the minimum outdoor air damper and return air damper fully; close the economizer outdoor air damper. 2. Measure outdoor airflow. 3. If outdoor airflow rate is above design minimum (DesVot for ASHRAE Standard 62.1 or DesMinOA for California Title 24), adjust damper linkage on minimum outdoor air damper so that intake is at design minimum with damper fully stroked. 4. If outdoor airflow rate is below design minimum, temporarily adjust return air damper position via the BAS until design outdoor airflow is achieved. This position shall be used for testing only and shall not limit the return air damper position during normal operation. 5. Note DP across the outdoor air damper. This value becomes the design minimum outdoor air DP setpoint DesMinDP in the BAS. Convey this setpoint to BAS installer and note on air balance report.

evidence.evidence_text: Instructions for establishing MinDP are given in the TAB specification. For example: 1. Open the minimum outdoor air damper and return air damper fully; close the economizer outdoor air damper. 2. Measure outdoor airflow. 3. If outdoor airflow rate is above design minimum (DesVot for ASHRAE Standard 62.1 or DesMinOA for California Title 24), adjust damper linkage on minimum outdoor air damper so that intake is at design minimum with damper fully stroked. 4. If outdoor airflow rate is below design minimum, temporarily adjust return air damper position via the BAS until design outdoor airflow is achieved. This position shall be used for testing only and shall not limit the return air damper position during normal operation. 5. Note DP across the outdoor air damper. This value becomes the design minimum outdoor air DP setpoint DesMinDP in the BAS. Convey this setpoint to BAS installer and note on air balance report.

manual_entries.title: TAB – Minimum Head Pressure Control Valve Position (Constant Speed CW Pumps)
manual_entries.summary: Establish MinCWVlvPos by configuring plant for lowest chiller stage, throttling head pressure control valve until flow through chiller condenser drops to 110% of minimum flow switch setpoint. Record valve position.

structured_payload.title: TAB – Minimum Head Pressure Control Valve Position (Constant Speed CW Pumps)
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Water-cooled chiller plant with constant speed CW pumps
structured_payload.summary: Establish MinCWVlvPos by configuring plant for lowest chiller stage, throttling head pressure control valve until flow through chiller condenser drops to 110% of minimum flow switch setpoint. Record valve position.
structured_payload.commissioning_check: Verify MinCWVlvPos is established per TAB: configure lowest stage, throttle valve until flow is 110% of minimum, record position.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: For constant speed pumps, instructions for establishing MinCWVlvPos should be provided in the Test and Balance Specification. For example: 1. Configure the plant for the lowest stage of chiller operation. Open the head pressure control valve of the test chiller and enable a CW pump and the corresponding number of cooling towers for that stage. 2. Throttle the head pressure control valve of the test chiller in 2% increments until the flow rate through the chiller’s condenser decreases to 110% of the minimum condenser water flow switch setpoint as determined by condenser differential pressure. 3. Note the valve position setpoint and report to controls contractor.

manual_entries.title: Equipment Staging and Rotation – Lead/Lag
manual_entries.summary: Lead/lag equipment shall be alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that equipment with most Staging Runtime is last stage and least is lead. Equipment are not hot-swapped during operation.

structured_payload.title: Equipment Staging and Rotation – Lead/Lag
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Parallel staged equipment (pumps, cooling towers, etc.)
structured_payload.summary: Lead/lag equipment shall be alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that equipment with most Staging Runtime is last stage and least is lead. Equipment are not hot-swapped during operation.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Lead/lag equipment: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment. This strategy effectively makes it such that equipment are not “hot swapped”, e.g., a pump would not be started and another stopped during operation just for runtime equalization.

chunk.cleaned_text:
Lead/lag equipment: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment  (such as CHW
pumps and cooling towers ) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one
is on so that the equipment  with the most operating hours  as determined by Staging Runtime  is
made the last stage equipment  and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage
equipment .
This strategy effectively makes it such that equipment are not “hot swapped”, e.g., a pump would
not be started and another stopped during operation just for runtime equalization.
For example, assume there are two equipment and only one is on, but the operating equipment has
exceeded the run hours of the disabled equipment. The equipment will not rotate positions until
either a stage up or down occurs.
chunk.text_excerpt:
Lead/lag equipment: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment  (such as CHW
pumps and cooling towers ) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one
is on s

evidence.evidence_text: Lead/lag equipment: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment. This strategy effectively makes it such that equipment are not “hot swapped”, e.g., a pump would not be started and another stopped during operation just for runtime equalization.

manual_entries.title: Chiller Plant Enable Logic
manual_entries.summary: Enable the chiller plant in the lowest stage when disabled for at least 15 minutes, number of Chiller Plant Requests exceeds I, OAT exceeds CH-LOT, and the enable schedule is active.

structured_payload.title: Chiller Plant Enable Logic
structured_payload.summary: Enable the chiller plant in the lowest stage when disabled for at least 15 minutes, number of Chiller Plant Requests exceeds I, OAT exceeds CH-LOT, and the enable schedule is active.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes, Number of Chiller Plant Requests > I (default 0, adjustable), OAT > CH-LOT, and chiller plant enable schedule is active.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Enable the plant in the lowest stage.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests > I (I = Ignores shall default to 0, adjustable), and b. OAT>CH-LOT, and c. The chiller plant enable schedule is active.

manual_entries.title: Chiller Plant Disable Logic
manual_entries.summary: Disable the chiller plant when enabled for at least 15 minutes and either number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, OAT < CH-LOT – 1°F, or the enable schedule is inactive.

structured_payload.title: Chiller Plant Disable Logic
structured_payload.summary: Disable the chiller plant when enabled for at least 15 minutes and either number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, OAT < CH-LOT – 1°F, or the enable schedule is inactive.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Plant has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and (Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes OR OAT < CH-LOT – 1°F OR chiller plant enable schedule is inactive).
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT<CH-LOT – 1°F, or c. The chiller plant enable schedule is inactive.

5.20.2.3.  Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and:
a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 mi nutes, or
b. OAT<CH -LOT – 1°F, or
c. The chiller plant enable schedule is inactive.
Chiller Plant Requests are generated by coil control valves. If the plant serves critical valves whose positions are
not known to the plant controller, e.g., pneumatic controls,  the Chiller Plant Request variable can be set to 1
manually by the operator such that the plant is enabled strictly based on OAT lockout and schedule per subsequent
logic.
At a future date, Importance multipliers (IM) shall be added to Chiller Plant Reque sts in AHU and fan coils
sequences to ensure that critical coils can independently cause the plant to start.

manual_entries.title: Waterside Economizer Enable Logic
manual_entries.summary: Enable waterside economizer if it has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT upstream of HX is greater than the predicted heat exchanger leaving water temperature (PHXLWT) plus 2°F.

structured_payload.title: Waterside Economizer Enable Logic
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Chilled Water Plant with waterside economizer
structured_payload.summary: Enable waterside economizer if it has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT upstream of HX is greater than the predicted heat exchanger leaving water temperature (PHXLWT) plus 2°F.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: WSE has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT upstream of HX > PHXLWT + 2°F.
configurable_values: PHXLWT (calculated from wetbulb, design approaches, and part load ratio)
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Enable waterside economizer (WSE) if it has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT upstream of HX is greater than the predicted heat exchanger leaving water temperature (PHXLWT) plus 2°F.

5.20.3.  Waterside Economizer Control
5.20.3.1.  Enable waterside economizer (WSE) if it has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT
upstream of HX is greater than the predicted heat exchanger leaving water temperature (PHXLWT)
plus 2°F. PHXLWT is:
𝑃𝐻𝑋𝐿𝑊𝑇 =𝑇𝑊𝐵+𝑃𝐴 𝐻𝑋+𝑃𝐴 𝐶𝑇
𝑃𝐴 𝐻𝑋=𝐷𝐴 𝐻𝑋∗𝑃𝐿𝑅 𝐻𝑋
𝑃𝐴 𝐶𝑇=𝑚∗(𝐷𝑇 𝑊𝐵−𝑇𝑊𝐵)+𝐷𝐴 𝐶𝑇
where
𝑇𝑊𝐵= current wetbul b temperature
𝑃𝐴 𝐻𝑋= predicted heat exchanger approach
𝑃𝐴 𝐶𝑇 = predicted cooling tower approach
𝐷𝐴 𝐻𝑋 = design heat exchanger approach
Retain the following PLR HX definition for primary -only plants. Delete otherwise.
𝑃𝐿𝑅 𝐻𝑋= the lesser of 1 and predicted heat exchanger part load ratio (current chilled water flow
rate divided by design HX chilled water flow rate)
Retain the following PLR HX definition for primary -secondary plants.
chunk.text_excerpt:
Disable the operating primary CHW pump(s) per Sections 5.20.6 .
Retain Section 5.20.3  if the plant has a waterside economizer. Delete otherwise.
5.20.3.  Waterside Economizer Control
5.20.3.1.  Enabl

evidence.evidence_text: Enable waterside economizer (WSE) if it has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT upstream of HX is greater than the predicted heat exchanger leaving water temperature (PHXLWT) plus 2°F.

manual_entries.title: Waterside Economizer Disable Logic
manual_entries.summary: Disable waterside economizer when it has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream of HX is greater than CHWRT upstream of HX less 1°F for 2 minutes.

structured_payload.title: Waterside Economizer Disable Logic
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Chilled Water Plant with waterside economizer
structured_payload.summary: Disable waterside economizer when it has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream of HX is greater than CHWRT upstream of HX less 1°F for 2 minutes.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: WSE has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream of HX > CHWRT upstream of HX – 1°F for 2 minutes.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Disable WSE when it has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream of HX is greater than CHWRT upstream of HX less 1ºF for 2 minutes (i.e., if the HX is not reducing the CHW temp by at least 1°F).

chunk.cleaned_text:
174 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.20.3.2.  Disable WSE when it has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream o f HX is greater
than CHWRT upstream of HX less 1ºF for 2 minutes (i.e., if the HX is not reducing the CHW temp
by at least 1°F).
5.20.3.3.  PHXLWT Tuning
a. Decrease “m” by 0.02 when the economizer is disabled if the economizer remained enabled for
greater than 60 minut es.
b. Increase “m” by 0.02 when the economizer is disabled if the economizer remained enabled for
less than 30 minutes and WseTower -MaxSpeed did not decrease below 100% speed while the
WSE was enabled. See Section 5.20.12.2.c.1.ii  for definition and use of WseTower -MaxSpeed.
c. “m” shall be limited to the range of -0.2 to 0.5.
d. “m” shall initialize at 0 upon first plant start up and shall not be reinitialized every time the
plant is disabled/enabled.

manual_entries.title: Chiller Minimum Stage Runtime
manual_entries.summary: Each chiller stage shall have a minimum runtime of 15 minutes before another stage change can occur.

structured_payload.title: Chiller Minimum Stage Runtime
structured_payload.summary: Each chiller stage shall have a minimum runtime of 15 minutes before another stage change can occur.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: A stage change has just completed.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Prevent any further stage changes for 15 minutes.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Each stage shall have a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.

a. Each stage shall have a minimum runtime of 15 minutes.
b. Timers shall reset to zero at the completion of every stage change.
c. Any unavailable stage (see Section 5.20.4.13 ) shall be skipped during staging events, but
staging conditionals in the current stage shall be evaluated as per usual.

Chiller staging for a Primary -only plant with a WSE mirrors staging for a standard Primary -only plant with the
only complications being de ciding (1) whether the plant can start with just the waterside economizer and (2) when
chillers can be staged off leaving the WSE to meet the load alone. For (1), the logic conservatively estimates the
CHWST that the WSE will be able to achieve assuming th e WSE HX is fully loaded at startup. If the WSE is
projected to be able to provide water at least 1°F colder than the CHWST setpoint, then the plant starts in WSE
mode.

chunk.cleaned_text:
Secondary CHW supply temperature > primary CHW supply temperature + 2°F for 10
minutes and the enabled primary pumps are at maximum speed; or
ii. Primary CHW supply temperature is 2°F > setpoint for 15 minutes.
o. Stage down if both of the following are true:
1. Next available lower stage OPLR  < SPLR DN for 15 minutes and next lower stage OPLR is
not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; and
2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true.
Primary -secondary staging needs to ensure that secondary flow does not exceed prima ry flow. When secondary flow
exceeds primary flow, the secondary CHWST degrades (elevates), in turn causing lower secondary CHWRT. This in
turn decreases the load on the chillers while causing the secondary flowrate to only increase further. Staging logic
avoids this positive feedback scenario by staging up when the presence of secondary recirculation has been
confirmed by secondary CHWST exceeding primary by 2°F.

minutes and the enabled primary pumps are at maximum speed; or

manual_entries.title: Chilled Water Supply Temperature Reset – Differential Pressure Controlled Loops
manual_entries.summary: Reset CHWST setpoint and pump DP setpoint based on a Trim & Respond loop. From 0-50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHW-DPmax. From 50-100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin.

structured_payload.title: Chilled Water Supply Temperature Reset – Differential Pressure Controlled Loops
structured_payload.summary: Reset CHWST setpoint and pump DP setpoint based on a Trim & Respond loop. From 0-50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHW-DPmax. From 50-100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Map CHW Plant Reset loop output to DP setpoint (0-50%) and CHWST setpoint (50-100%) as described.
configurable_values: Trim & Respond parameters (SP0, SPmin, SPmax, Td, T, I, SPtrim, SPres, SPres-max)
structured_payload.evidence_quote: a. From 0% loop output to 50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHWP-DPmax. b. From 50% loop output to 100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin.

chunk.cleaned_text:
198 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  a. From 0% loop output to 50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW -DPmin to CHWP -
DPmax.
b. From 50% loop output to 100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to
CHWSTmin.
c. CHW Plant Reset variable shall be reset using Trim & Respond logic with the following
parameters:
Variable  Value
Device  Any CHW Pump Distribution Loop
SP0 100%
SPmin 0%
SPmax 100%
Td 15 minutes
T 5 minutes
I 2
R Cooling CHWST Reset Requests
SPtrim -2%
SPres +3%
SPres-max +7%
The reset starts at CHWSTmin because starting at a high temperature often causes the chiller to bring down
CHWST too quickly and pass the CHWST setpoint, leading the chiller to cycle off.  Additionally, if the loop reset
starts at a CHWST that cannot satisfy the load at startup (e.g., CHWST setpoint = 60°F, but an AHU requires 55°F
supply air), there is a resultant delay in satisfying the load as the reset loop winds up before CHWST setpoin t resets
down.
d.
chunk.text_excerpt:
198 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  a. From 0% loop output to 50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW -DPmin to CHWP -
DPmax.
b. From 50% loop output to 100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWS

evidence.evidence_text: a. From 0% loop output to 50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHWP-DPmax. b. From 50% loop output to 100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin.

manual_entries.title: CHW Plant – Pump or Tower Fan Alarm
manual_entries.summary: Pump or tower fan alarm if status differs from command for 15 seconds: commanded on but status off is Level 2 (delay 15 sec); commanded off but status on is Level 4 (delay 60 sec).

structured_payload.title: CHW Plant – Pump or Tower Fan Alarm
structured_payload.summary: Pump or tower fan alarm if status differs from command for 15 seconds: commanded on but status off is Level 2 (delay 15 sec); commanded off but status on is Level 4 (delay 60 sec).
structured_payload.fault_condition: Status differs from command for 15 seconds.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Pump or tower fan alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command for 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been commanded on for 15 seconds. b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4. Do not evaluate the alarm until the equipment has been commanded off for 60 seconds.

manual_entries.title: CHW Plant – FC#6: Chilled Water Supply Temperature Too High
manual_entries.summary: Fault condition: CHWST AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP. Possible causes: mechanical problem with chillers, primary flow higher than design evaporator flow.

structured_payload.title: CHW Plant – FC#6: Chilled Water Supply Temperature Too High
structured_payload.summary: Fault condition: CHWST AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP. Possible causes: mechanical problem with chillers, primary flow higher than design evaporator flow.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: FC#6 Equation CHWST AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP Applies to OS #2 – #5 Description Chilled water supply temperature is too high Possible Diagnosis Mechanical problem with chillers Primary flow is higher than the design evaporator flow of the operating chillers

manual_entries.title: CHW Plant – FC#8: Condenser Approach Too High
manual_entries.summary: Fault condition: Approach COND ≥ RefrigCondTemp CH-x, AVG - CWRT CH-x, AVG. Possible causes: condenser fouling, blocked tubes, low CW temperature or flow.

structured_payload.title: CHW Plant – FC#8: Condenser Approach Too High
structured_payload.summary: Fault condition: Approach COND ≥ RefrigCondTemp CH-x, AVG - CWRT CH-x, AVG. Possible causes: condenser fouling, blocked tubes, low CW temperature or flow.
structured_payload.fault_condition: Approach COND ≥ RefrigCondTemp CH-x, AVG - CWRT CH-x, AVG
structured_payload.evidence_quote: FC#8 Equation Approach COND ≥ RefrigCondTemp CH-x, AVG - CWRT CH-x, AVG Applies to OS #2, #3, #5 Description Condenser approach is too high Possible Diagnosis Possible condenser fouling or blocked condenser tubes Low condenser water temperature Low condenser water flow

Delete otherwise.
FC#7  Equation  CHW -PGAUGE, AVG  < 0.9 * CHW -ETPreChargePress  Applies to
OS
#1 – #5 Description  Chilled water system gauge pressure is too low
Possible Diagnosis  Possible chilled water system leak
Retain the following fault condition for water -cooled plants with chiller network interfaces. Delete otherwise.
FC#8  Equation  Approach COND ≥ RefrigCondTemp CH-x, AVG - CWRT CH-x, AVG
Applies to
OS
#2, #3, #5  Description  Condenser approach is too high
Possible Diagnosis  Possible condenser fouling or blocked condenser tubes
Low condenser water temperature
Low condenser water flow
Retain the following fault condition for plants with chiller network interfaces.  Delete otherwise.
FC#9  Equation  Approach EVAP ≥ CHWST CH-x, AVG - RefrigEvapTemp CH-x, AVG
Applies to
OS
#2, #3, #5  Description  Evaporator approach is too high
Possible Diagnosis  Possible evaporator fouling or blocked evaporator tubes
Low refrigeration charge
Contaminated refrigeration charge.
